title: Murder at Blackwood Manor
subtitle: An LLM-powered detective mystery

setting.place: Blackwood Manor, an English country house
setting.weather: A stormy evening with heavy rain and wind

victim.name: Edward Blackwood
victim.description: Edward Blackwood was a powerful and controlling publisher whose wealth, cruelty, and secrets gave several people reasons to fear or resent him.

premise: Edward Blackwood is found murdered in his private library after dinner during a storm at Blackwood Manor. Every guest had a reason to want him dead: inheritance, romantic humiliation, family resentment, jealousy, or fear of exposure. The player must investigate the manor, examine clues, interview suspects, confront them with evidence, and make a final accusation.

player_role.title: Detective
player_role.description: The player acts as the investigator called to solve Edward Blackwood's murder. The player can inspect locations, discover clues, question suspects, confront suspects with evidence, review case notes, and make a final accusation.

main_objective: Discover who killed Edward Blackwood, why they killed him, how the murder was committed, and which evidence proves the accusation.
core_gameplay_loop: Review discovered clues in the case notes
Optionally confront suspects with discovered evidence
Use NPC responses to refine the investigation
Make a final accusation using culprit, motive, method, and evidence

clue_state_model.discovered_clues: Clues the player has found while inspecting locations. These appear in case notes and can be used in the final accusation.
clue_state_model.revealed_clues: Clues the player has explicitly used to confront a suspect during an interview. Suspects should react directly only to evidence revealed to them.

input_types: optional confronted clue selected from discovered clues
npc_response_context: clues already revealed to this suspect

rag_model.retrieval_usage: Relevant context is retrieved during suspect interviews to ground NPC responses. Retrieved context must be filtered by suspect, discovered clues, confronted evidence, and current game state so the LLM does not reveal the solution too early.

langgraph_model.usage: LangGraph orchestrates the NPC interview workflow.
workflow_steps: Return NPC response to the Streamlit interface
langgraph_model.reason: The suspect interview flow is a multi-step stateful process. LangGraph provides a clear structure for coordinating evidence validation, RAG retrieval, prompt construction, response generation, consistency checking, and game state updates.

mvp_features: FAISS-based retrieval for clues, character memories, timeline facts, and dialogue context
Prompt engineering for constrained NPC behavior
stretch_goals: Free-form final accusation evaluated by an LLM
More advanced consistency checker using a second LLM call
MCP integration for dynamic case generation, external case-file management, save/load tools, and multi-agent game-master orchestration
